REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT RSM&A FORECLOSURE SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CANCELLATION OF INSTRUMENTS. NO LEAVE TO AMEND WAS PREVIOUSLY GRANTED. DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS RE: DUTY, BREACH OF DUTY AND DELAYED DISCOVERY. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT PURPORTS TO DEMUR TO OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AND BASED ON OTHER ARGUMENTS, AS THESE ISSUES ARE NOT PROPERTY OUTLINED IN THE NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER. SPECIFICALLY, ARGUMENTS APPEARING IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, BUT NOT IN THE NOTICE PERTAIN TO (1) "SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY" (1:27); (2) EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE" (2:7); (3) "CAUSE OF ACTIN FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF" (2:12); (4) FAILURE TO INCLUDE AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY (10:3-26). ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK:N. WONG, NOT REPORTED, D501.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT: WITH AN EXCEPTION NOTED BELOW, ALTAPACIFIC BANK'S AND DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP'S SUMMARY ADJUDICATION/SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS DIRECTED TO PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE DENIED. BOTH ALTAPACIFIC'S AND DOLMEN'S MOTIONS ARE PREMISED ON ASSERTIONS THAT A 2007 DEED OF TRUST ENCUMBERED BOTH 637 PERALTA AND 639 PERALTA. HOWEVER, IN DENYING ANOTHER DEFENDANT'S SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION ON MAY 10, 2016, THE COURT ALREADY FOUND TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO, INTER ALIA, WHETHER A LEGAL DESCRIPTION WAS ATTACHED TO THE DEED OF TRUST ONLY AFTER PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE 2007 DEED. 5/10/16 ORDER P. 5 (THAT ORDER IS INCORPORATED HERE BY REFERENCE). THAT COULD VOID THE DEED OF TRUST AS TO 639 PERALTA. WHEN "A DEED IS ALTERED OR CHANGED BY SOMEONE OTHER THAN THE GRANTOR BEFORE IT IS DELIVERED OR RECORDED, AND THE ALTERATION IS WITHOUT THE GRANTOR'S KNOWLEDGE OR CONSENT, THE DEED IS VOID AND NO TITLE VESTS IN THE GRANTEE OR SUBSEQUENT PURCHASERS, EVEN BONA FIDE PURCHASERS FOR VALUE." 3 MILLER & STARR, CAL. REAL ESTATE (3D ED. 2011) 8:53; MONTGOMERY, 85 CA2D AT 563; LIN, 232 CA4TH AT 714-15. NEITHER THE EXCERPTS OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION PRESENTED WITH DOLMEN'S REPLY NOR ATTACHMENT OF THE DEED TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS A "JUDICIAL ADMISSION." PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT COUNT 7 OF HIS COMPLAINT DOES NOT APPLY TO ALTAPACIFIC, SO ALTAPACIFIC'S MOTION IS GRANTED TO THAT LIMITED EXTENT. DOLMEN FILED ONLY A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: ANGLEA POURTABIB, CSR#13714, (415)433-5777. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ALTAPACIFIC BANK, A CALIFORNIA STATE CHARTED NON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: WITH AN EXCEPTION NOTED BELOW, ALTAPACIFIC BANK'S AND DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP'S SUMMARY ADJUDICATION/SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS DIRECTED TO PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE DENIED. BOTH ALTAPACIFIC'S AND DOLMEN'S MOTIONS ARE PREMISED ON ASSERTIONS THAT A 2007 DEED OF TRUST ENCUMBERED BOTH 637 PERALTA AND 639 PERALTA. HOWEVER, IN DENYING ANOTHER DEFENDANT'S SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION ON MAY 10, 2016, THE COURT ALREADY FOUND TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO, INTER ALIA, WHETHER A LEGAL DESCRIPTION WAS ATTACHED TO THE DEED OF TRUST ONLY AFTER PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE 2007 DEED. 5/10/16 ORDER P. 5 (THAT ORDER IS INCORPORATED HERE BY REFERENCE). THAT COULD VOID THE DEED OF TRUST AS TO 639 PERALTA. WHEN "A DEED IS ALTERED OR CHANGED BY SOMEONE OTHER THAN THE GRANTOR BEFORE IT IS DELIVERED OR RECORDED, AND THE ALTERATION IS WITHOUT THE GRANTOR'S KNOWLEDGE OR CONSENT, THE DEED IS VOID AND NO TITLE VESTS IN THE GRANTEE OR SUBSEQUENT PURCHASERS, EVEN BONA FIDE PURCHASERS FOR VALUE." 3 MILLER & STARR, CAL. REAL ESTATE (3D ED. 2011) 8:53; MONTGOMERY, 85 CA2D AT 563; LIN, 232 CA4TH AT 714-15. NEITHER THE EXCERPTS OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION PRESENTED WITH DOLMEN'S REPLY NOR ATTACHMENT OF THE DEED TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS A "JUDICIAL ADMISSION." PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT COUNT 7 OF HIS COMPLAINT DOES NOT APPLY TO ALTAPACIFIC, SO ALTAPACIFIC'S MOTION IS GRANTED TO THAT LIMITED EXTENT. DOLMEN FILED ONLY A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: ANGLEA POURTABIB, CSR#13714, (415)433-5777. (501)

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY AND BREACH OF CONTRACT. ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE SUFFICIENT TO STATE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION, TO SHOW THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT BARRED BY THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT OR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DECLARATORY RELIEF OPERATES PROSPECTIVELY, SERVING TO SET CONTROVERSIES AT REST AND IS NOT AVAILABLE WHERE ONLY PAST WRONGS ARE INVOLVED, I.E. WHERE THERE IS AN ACCRUED CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AN ACTUAL BREACH OF CONTRACT OR OTHER WRONGFUL ACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT RSM&A FORECLOSURE SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

Appearances: Brian H. Newman of Dykema Gossett PLLC (213.457.1800 x1731) appearing via Court Call for defendant and opposing party; Erin N. Collins of Manning & Kass Ellrod Ramirez Trester LLP (415.400.0818) for cross-defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for equitable indemnity and breach of contract. Allegations in the complaint are sufficient to state these causes of action, to show that these causes of action are not barred by the good faith settlement or statute of limitations. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Declaratory relief operates prospectively, serving to set controversies at rest and is not available where only past wrongs are involved, i.e. where there is an accrued cause of action for an actual breach of contract or other wrongful act. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/AJR)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
David Dolman, (415)644-4828 for Defendant/Moving party Dolmen Property Groupt LLC.
Mark Epstein, (510)834-6600 for Defendant/Moving party Altapacific Bank.
Eric Shaw, (415)536-2800 for Plaintiff/Opposing party William Bradley.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ALTAPACIFIC BANK, A CALIFORNIA STATE CHARTED NON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication: With an exception noted below, AltaPacific Bank's and Dolmen Property Group's summary adjudication/summary judgment motions directed to plaintiff's fourth amended complaint are DENIED. Both AltaPacific's and Dolmen's motions are premised on assertions that a 2007 deed of trust encumbered both 637 Peralta and 639 Peralta. However, in denying another defendant's summary adjudication motion on May 10, 2016, the Court already found triable issues of fact as to, inter alia, whether a legal description was attached to the deed of trust only after plaintiff signed the 2007 deed. 5/10/16 Order p. 5 (that order is incorporated here by reference). That could void the deed of trust as to 639 Peralta. When "a deed is altered or changed by someone other than the grantor before it is delivered or recorded, and the alteration is without the grantor's knowledge or consent, the deed is void and no title vests in the grantee or subsequent purchasers, even bona fide purchasers for value." 3 Miller & Starr, Cal. Real Estate (3d ed. 2011) 8:53; Montgomery, 85 CA2d at 563; Lin, 232 CA4th at 714-15. Neither the excerpts of plaintiff's deposition presented with Dolmen's reply nor attachment of the deed to plaintiff's complaint is a "judicial admission."
Plaintiff concedes that count 7 of his complaint does not apply to AltaPacific, so AltaPacific's motion is GRANTED to that limited extent. Dolmen filed only a summary judgment motion. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Anglea Pourtabib, CSR#13714, (415)433-5777. (501)
